overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, focusing on research related to computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated online behavior. His LinkedIn profile has the largest follower base, while Bluesky shows higher engagement in terms of reposts. Overall, his content revolves around research findings, conference presentations, and job/research opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Sharing research findings, publications, and methodologies related to computational social science.
Focus on detecting, analyzing, and mitigating disinformation and coordinated inauthentic behavior online.
Announcing postdoctoral positions, PhD opportunities, and grant awards related to social media research.
Promoting conference participation and presenting research at academic events.

recommendations: Increase cross-platform promotion of content to maximize reach and engagement.
Tailor content to each platform's audience to optimize relevance and engagement.
Encourage more interaction by posing questions and soliciting opinions in posts.
Use relevant hashtags consistently to improve content discoverability.
Engage with followers in the comments and replies to foster a stronger community.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows relatively high engagement in terms of reposts compared to likes. Content related to publications and research opportunities performs well. Experiment with interactive content to further boost engagement.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has a smaller following compared to other platforms. Content sharing research tools and discussing methodological approaches receive moderate engagement. Focus on creating more engaging content tailored to the AOIR community.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower base, and content related to research grants and job postings generates the most engagement. Continue sharing announcements and professional opportunities to leverage this network.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
